output.desc: True iff the paper either (a) proposes/studies a technique that requires open weights or inference-stack control to apply (quantization like GPTQ/AWQ, LoRA/QLoRA finetuning, speculative decoding internals, mechanistic interpretability / SAEs, structured-output / constrained decoding, on-device inference) OR (b) provides a substantive argument for running LLMs locally (privacy, data sovereignty, regulation, federated learning over LLMs). False for papers that merely use a closed LLM via API, RAG over closed APIs, prompt-only jailbreaks, closed-vs-open benchmarks, and inference-system work whose benefit accrues mainly to closed providers.
